top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
64ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At mile 60 of 99, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge's paved state highways circumnavigating Lake Taupō forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 99 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
At Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
6,407ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,407ft of climbing on paved state highways circumnavigating Lake Taupō does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 99 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
6,407ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved state highways circumnavigating Lake Taupō. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge’s 99 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, TT Threshold, and Durability to prepare you for 99 miles of paved state highways circumnavigating lake taupō in Taupō, Waikato, New Zealand.
